expectations.evidence: app/main.py:26 — kelet.configure(api_key=settings.kelet_api_key, project=settings.kelet_project) at top of lifespan, gated on settings.kelet_api_key only (no AND on project).

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: src/routers/chat.py:41 — async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wraps the entire _run_agent_stream body including the [DONE] sentinel.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: frontend/src/main.tsx:13 — <KeletProvider apiKey={apiKey} project={project}> wrapping <App />; apiKey read from import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: frontend/src/App.tsx:58 — <VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}> inside AiMessage component, rendered next to each AI bubble. asChild on Up/Down to avoid nested <button>.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: App.tsx:121 reads res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') → setSessionId(headerSessionId); same sessionId state passed to VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId} and CopyButton's useKeletSignal({session_id: sessionId, ...}).

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env at end of integration (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: .env has KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-eval-test + KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-iter8-react; frontend/.env has VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk-kelet-eval-test + VITE_KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-iter8-react. Neither hardcoded in source.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.
expectations.evidence: VoteFeedback + copy buttons styled with existing palette tokens (--text-muted, --panel, --border, --accent) defined in styles.css. Pill buttons with 999px radius, 12px muted text, active state uses --accent border — matches dark chat theme.

expectations.text: Skill considers at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback (e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or session abandon) — either wiring it, or explicitly proposing it in the plan and deferring with a rationale. A silent omission (no mention at all) fails; an informed 'deferred to keep scaffold minimal' passes.
expectations.evidence: Copy-to-clipboard button using useKeletSignal wired in App.tsx:13-36 — emits {kind: 'EVENT', source: 'HUMAN', trigger_name: 'user-copy'} on copy.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator creation runs via Bash — curl POST to /api/projects/<project>/synthetics against localhost:8765 (per eval environment override). Transcript must show the localhost URL, not api.kelet.ai. Not shown as a copy-paste code block for the developer.
expectations.evidence: curl -sS --max-time 360 -X POST 'http://localhost:8765/api/projects/docs-ai-iter8-react/synthetics' with sk-kelet-eval-test bearer → created=4 updated=0 failed=0 deduped=false / 200.

expectations.text: At Checkpoint 2, the skill asks the three-option API key mode AskUserQuestion (paste secret key / I'll grab one / can't paste secrets) when synthetic evaluators were selected.
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md and transcript.md both record the three-option key mode question with 'Paste secret key (sk-kelet-...)' selected.

expectations.text: kelet.shutdown() is called at process teardown — inside FastAPI lifespan finally: block (or equivalent). Without it, BatchSpanProcessor drops buffered spans on pod rotation. Grep the diff for 'kelet.shutdown()' paired with a 'finally:' in app/main.py or wherever lifespan is defined.
expectations.evidence: Diff: finally: → await docs_cache.stop() → await redis.aclose() → kelet.shutdown(). Inside FastAPI lifespan in app/main.py.
